query: A travelling salesman only eats ramen and fries. Each pack of ramen contains calories, grams of protein, and mg of sodium. Each pack of fries contains calories, grams of protein, and mg of sodium. Since fries are easier to eat while driving, at most % of his meals can be ramen. The salesman wants to ensure he eats at least calories and grams of protein. How many of each should he eat to minimize his sodium intake?
